title: Erosion and Earth's Surface Study Guide

contentProfile.primarySkill: Understanding erosion processes and map reading
contentProfile.assignmentFormat: Study guide with multiple concept sections
sourceEvidence: matched science term: erosion
matched science term: landform
matched science term: landforms
matched science term: soil
matched science term: rocks
matched science term: water
matched science term: wind
Erosion happens when wind, water, or ice wear away rocks and soil
Rivers and rain can carry tiny pieces of rock from mountains to beaches or oceans
The color of sand comes from the rocks nearby
Water and wind break down the rocks and move them to the beach
Maps show land and water like mountains, rivers, lakes, and oceans
A compass rose shows directions: North, South, East, West
Rivers usually flow downhill from mountains to oceans or lakes
Rivers start high in the mountains
They flow down to lower places like lakes or oceans
Water always flows downhill, never up
If you float on a river, you'll end up in a lake or ocean
Rounded hills are smaller and softer than pointy peaks
Pointy peaks are tall and sharp — like big mountains
Use clues and directions (North, South, East, West) to find locations on a map

capturedContent.title: Erosion and Earth's Surface Study Guide

questions.question: What is erosion?
questions.correctAnswer: Erosion happens when wind, water, or ice wear away rocks and soil
questions.hint: Think about what wears away rocks and soil over time

questions.question: Where do rivers and rain carry tiny pieces of rock?
questions.correctAnswer: From mountains to beaches or oceans
questions.hint: Think about the journey water takes from high places to low places

questions.question: Why is beach sand a certain color?
questions.correctAnswer: The color of sand comes from the rocks nearby
questions.hint: Think about what breaks down to become sand

questions.question: What do the four directions on a compass rose show?
questions.hint: A compass rose helps us find locations on a map

questions.question: Which direction do rivers flow?
questions.correctAnswer: Downhill, from mountains to oceans or lakes
questions.hint: Water always flows downhill, never up

questions.question: What is the difference between rounded hills and pointy peaks?
questions.correctAnswer: Rounded hills are smaller and softer than pointy peaks. Pointy peaks are tall and sharp — like big mountains
questions.hint: Look at the shapes and sizes of landforms on maps
